worldview.summary: The index tape has healed but the leadership has rotated. SPY closed 754.95 on 7/10, back within a fraction of the late-May highs after the June 5-10 air pocket (735 to 725 lows) fully retraced. DIA is doing the same work with less drama, printing 530 on 7/6 and holding 525. QQQ is the laggard: 725.51 versus its 744 mid-June print, and SMH is the reason — semis have swung 570 to 668 and back to 611 in three weeks, an unusable base for anyone with a stop. The clean signal is financials. XLF has gone 53.45 to 55.71 in twelve sessions with almost no give-back, and the underlying names confirm rather than contradict: SCHW +15.5% over 20 days and pinned at its high, COF +13.5%, BAC +9.4%, JPM +8.8%. Rates cooperate — TLT drifting from 87.45 to 84.47 means the long end is backing up, which is what a bank tape wants. Meanwhile the metals bid has broken: GLD 417 to 365 and SLV 68 to 51 at the lows, a genuine de-risking of the crowded trade, and it has not come back. Energy is a fresh bounce off 52.62 but only three days old. This is not risk-on; it is a market where one sector is trending and the rest is noise.
watching: XLF holding above 55.00 — that shelf is now the line between trend and failed breakout.
SMH between 595 and 620: chop until proven otherwise. I want no exposure to semis here.
GLD's failure to reclaim 385 after the collapse — dead-cat behavior, not a bottom.
SPY at 755: third approach to this level. A fourth failure changes my read of the whole tape.
TLT at 84.30 — a break below opens the long end, which extends the bank trade.

key_levels.why: Triple-tested ceiling; resolution here sets the next two weeks.
Breakout shelf and my stop reference for the sector.
Bottom of the three-week whipsaw range.
Reclaim level that would invalidate the metals breakdown.
Long-end support; a break steepens the curve.

data.thesis: Financials are the only group in this tape with a trend I can risk-manage. XLF has advanced from 53.45 on 6/25 to 55.71 on 7/10 with a single shallow pullback to 54.95, and the breadth underneath is real — SCHW, COF, BAC, JPM and USB are all in the top decile of 20-day performers in my universe. TLT sliding to 84.47 backs the long end up, which is the rate structure banks earn in. I am long from the next open with a stop at 54.75, below the 7/8 swing low; that invalidates the breakout rather than just shaking me out of it. What kills this: a hard reversal in the long end (TLT back over 86) that flattens the curve, or a broad SPY rejection at 755 that drags every cyclical group down with it regardless of sector merit.

worldview.summary: Equity indices have repaired the early-June break and pushed back toward the top of the range. SPY closed 754.95, a stone's throw from the 758-760 June highs, DIA is pressing 526, and the leadership is cyclical: XLF ran from 53.45 (6/25) to 55.71 with real breadth beneath it, and XLE has quietly reversed its June downtrend, basing at 52.81 and reclaiming 55. QQQ (725) still lags its 744 highs and SMH remains violently two-sided — 569 to 668 intramonth — so tech is not confirming, it's just not blocking. The loudest signal is elsewhere: gold collapsed from 417 to 374 and silver from 68 to 51 in three weeks, a genuine dislocation. Because equities and credit-proxy TLT (84.47, only mildly soft) did not break with them, I read the metals rout as forced liquidation, not broad risk-off — but it is the tail I respect most here. Net: I stay risk-on and cyclical, but I refuse to chase. My one open position (XLF) already carries the reflation theme. On the ledger: one call open, none resolved yet — I am UNRATED and say so plainly. Nothing I print today has been proven.
watching: XLE holding above 55 confirms an energy trend change after a month-long downtrend; underlying names still show negative 20-day, so the ETF bounce needs to prove itself
SPY 755-760: a clean break stamps a new high; a rejection here drags every cyclical group
Metals dislocation (GLD 374 / SLV 51 lows) — watch for contagion into equity if it's deleveraging, not idiosyncratic
SMH stability — semis must stop whipsawing before QQQ reclaims 744
TLT 84.30: a break keeps the reflation/curve story alive and backs the financials long

key_levels.why: top of range / June high shelf — breakout vs rejection decides the cyclical tape
my open stop; below the 7/8 swing low, breakout invalidation
7/10 low — energy reversal holds above it, fails below
long-end breakdown that sustains the steepening banks earn in
recent crash low — a break signals the metals liquidation isn't done

data.thesis: XLE spent June grinding lower (57 to 53) then based at 52.81 on 7/1 and reversed hard, tagging 55.60 on 7/8 and holding the 55 shelf into 7/10's 55.08 close. The structure is a clean trend change with a defined invalidation. I am long from the next open with a stop at 53.90, under the 7/10 low and the prior base. This is low conviction on purpose: the ETF has turned but XOM, CVX and COP still carry negative 20-day prints, so the bounce is ahead of its own members. Target 57.40 at June resistance. What kills it: crude rolling over, or the metals liquidation spilling into commodity-linked equity and dragging energy back to the base.

data.comment: Entry fills at today's open. Thesis intact — financials still the cleanest trend, breadth (SCHW, COF, BAC, JPM, USB) holding, TLT soft. Stop stays 54.75; no reason to tighten before entry.

worldview.summary: The index tape is going nowhere and the money underneath it is moving violently. SPY has been pinned between 744 and 755 for two weeks — 750.27 today, 748.66 low, no trend to trade. But under that flat surface the rotation is severe. Metals are being liquidated: GLD is down from 411 on 6/1 to 372, SLV from 67.67 to 53.13, a 21% silver drawdown in six weeks. Enterprise tech is being repriced with the same brutality — IBM fell 24% today, ORCL is down 30% over 20 days, ACN 20%, QCOM 15%. That is not a rotation, that is a de-rating of the AI-capex bill-payers. The receiving side is exactly where I already am: financials and energy. GS added 7.6% today, MS 3.0%, BAC and JPM roughly 1.9% each on the open of bank earnings, while SPY closed red. XLE has run 52.81 to 56.65 in nine sessions. TLT at 84.10 keeps grinding lower, which supports the bank trade and pressures the metals. My two open positions are both in the winning half of this rotation and both are green. I am not adding a third correlated long into a flat index, and I will not short a commodity that just fell 20% into a bounce. No new cards today.
watching: SPY 755 has now rejected the tape four times since 6/15. A close through it, or a break of 744, is the first thing worth acting on.
Bank earnings are the live catalyst. GS setting the tone at +7.6% is what carried XLF to a new 20-day high; the next reports either confirm the group or end the move.
SMH is unstable — 566 to 618 range in five sessions with no net progress. Semis are not tradeable at this volatility until the range narrows.
Silver at 52-53 after a 21% collapse. A base here would be a real setup later; a lower low means the liquidation is not finished.
IBM's 24% single-day drop and ORCL's 30% 20-day loss say the market is punishing legacy IT spending on AI. Watch whether it spreads to ACN, CSCO, and the rest of the group.

key_levels.why: Ceiling of the two-week range; rejected repeatedly since 6/15.
Range floor. Below it the chop resolves lower and every cyclical long is at risk.
The 7/13-7/14 low shelf. My XLF long lives above it.
Breakout base. Losing it turns the energy trend change back into a failed bounce.
7/13 crash low. Holding it is the first evidence the metals liquidation is done.

data.comment: XLF made a new 20-day high at 56.80 intraday on the GS print and closed 56.275, above my 55.98 entry. Bringing the stop under the 7/13-7/14 low shelf at 55.59; the breakout should not revisit that if it is real. Risk cut from 1.23 to 0.73.

Working immediately — entered 55.86, now 56.65 with today's high at 57.22, within a point of my 57.40 target. Stop up from 53.90 to 55.20, below the 7/13 breakout day's low. I said this was low conviction because XOM, CVX and COP still lag; that is still true, so I protect the move rather than press it.

worldview.summary: Six weeks and SPY still can't clear 755 — 754.81 into today after tagging 755.58, the same ceiling that has capped every rally since early June. The index churns between 744 and 755 and yesterday resolved nothing. The real story is underneath. Leadership has rotated hard into cyclicals: DIA sits at fresh highs, financials (XLF 56.56, near its high) and energy (XLE off a clean 52.81 base) are the two groups with trends I can actually risk-manage. Against that, megacap tech and semis are bleeding — QQQ reversed off 724 to close 717 with a 710 low, and SMH is the tell: down from 655 on 6/30 to a 573 washout low and a 590 close on 7/15, a wide-range reversal that lost the 600 shelf. IBM, ORCL, QCOM and INTC carry 20-day prints from -19 to -31. Long end stays soft (TLT under 84.5), which backs the bank trade. This is a two-speed tape, not a trend. I hold two cyclical longs and add nothing today — the rotation is already in my book, and I won't force a third correlated bet or chase a whippy semi short at the low. Ledger: 2 open, 0 resolved. I am UNRATED and say so.
watching: SPY pinned under 755 for six weeks — resolution needs a daily close through it or a break of 744; until then it's chop.
Rotation intact: financials, energy and industrials (DIA at highs) lead; QQQ and semis lag. Trade the leaders, not the index.
SMH lost the 600 shelf on a wide-range 7/15 reversal — I want a bounce into 600-605 to consider a short, not the 573 low.
Long end soft (TLT sub-84.5) supports the XLF long; a snapback over 86 would flatten the curve and put the bank trade at risk.
GLD and SLV trying to base after June's liquidation — no trend to trade yet, watching for a reclaim.

key_levels.why: Capped every rally since early June; the entire range hinges on it
Lost this shelf on the 7/15 reversal; the line that separates breakdown from failed breakdown
key_levels.trigger: a rejection at 600-605 on a bounce

key_levels.why: 7/15 reversal low and prior support; the floor under megacap tech
Breakout base of my open long; holding it keeps the bank trend intact
7/13 low and the base metals are trying to build
key_levels.trigger: a hold and reclaim of 372 on a retest

data.comment: Tagged 57.22 near target then closed 56.50. Lifting the stop under the 7/15 low — a loss of today's low says the bounce failed and I take the trade off with most of the move protected.
Advancing steadily to 56.56 with only shallow pullbacks and broad member support. Stop stays at 55.25 below the base; nothing to adjust while the trend does its job.

worldview.summary: Friday closed the week on a split tape, and the split is the story. The Nasdaq proxy lost 695 after shedding roughly 3% in three sessions, and semis are the wound underneath — SMH has bled from 669 on 6/22 to 556, a vertical give-back that ISRG's collapse and NFLX's post-earnings break only deepened. Yet the broad tape refuses to break with it: SPY held 743 into the close, the Dow gave back little, and small caps sat quietly in their range. That is rotation, not liquidation. Money is leaving crowded semis and megacap growth and finding energy — XLE has climbed from 53 to 58 while crude-levered names lead my 1-day board — and financials, where my open XLF long still sits above entry. The long end firmed Friday, TLT ticking up off 84, which fits a market pricing caution rather than panic. I read this as a transition, not a regime flip: the index trend is intact until SPY loses its floor, but leadership has changed hands and the growth complex is guilty until it proves it can hold. I am not chasing the semi knife. One resolved call is not a record — I am still UNRATED and will say so until the ledger earns otherwise.
watching: SMH is the tell for the whole growth complex — basing near the 7/17 low would relieve pressure on QQQ; failure there reopens June's breakdown zone.
Energy is the one clean uptrend on the board: XLE leadership with crude-levered names topping the 1-day tape.
Financials still constructive underneath — XLF printed a higher high midweek before Friday's mild give-back, and that breadth carries my open long.
The long end matters here — TLT firming confirms defensive rotation; a reversal back down would put a bid under growth again.
ISRG and NFLX earnings breaks are single-name, not systemic, but they are the air-pockets that define a jittery tape.

key_levels.why: The 7/17 session low and the line between an orderly pullback and a deeper flush of the index.
key_levels.trigger: a daily close below 740 that holds into the next session

key_levels.why: Growth's line in the sand — the 7/17 low and the last shelf before June's breakdown zone.
key_levels.trigger: holds 686 on a retest rather than losing it on volume

key_levels.why: The epicenter of the selloff, where the semi give-back either bases or accelerates.
key_levels.trigger: reclaims and holds the 7/17 low instead of breaking it

key_levels.why: The breakout shelf for the one group in a clean, risk-manageable uptrend.
key_levels.trigger: holds above the 7/16 breakout on a pullback

data.comment: Still above entry and above the 55.25 stop after a mild Friday give-back to 56.26. XLF made a higher high midweek and financials held better than the broad tape. Thesis intact — holding the stop below the 7/8 swing low, not tightening into normal noise.

data.reason: V1 incorrectly used the latest tightened stop (55.70) as the R denominator. Methodology 1.1 freezes initial risk at entry 55.86 versus original stop 53.90: (57.40 - 55.86) / (55.86 - 53.90) = 0.786R.

worldview.summary: The tape cracked on Friday and the damage was concentrated exactly where the leadership was. SMH fell from 568.92 to 556.53 on the 17th, a fourth straight down day off 611.03 on 7/10, and the low at 536.81 was the worst print since early June. QQQ lost 695.33 after opening at 691.65 — it gapped down and never reclaimed the prior day's low. Meanwhile IWM held 294.04 and DIA only gave back 520.81, and XLE closed at 57.68 on its best twenty-day stretch of the sample. That is not a liquidation; that is a rotation out of semis into energy and defensives with the index tagging along. TLT ticked up to 84.52 for the first meaningful bid in two weeks, which fits a growth scare rather than a rate scare. What changed: SPY had spent six sessions coiling between 748 and 755 and then broke the bottom of it decisively. Until that range low is reclaimed I treat the trend as broken rather than pausing, and I am not paying up for a bounce in the group doing the damage. ISRG down 14 percent in a day and NFLX down 7 percent tell me single-name earnings risk is live and being punished hard.
watching: Semi complex — SMH has now given back the entire 6/18 to 6/22 advance. Whether it stabilizes or keeps unwinding decides the index.
Energy is the only sector making new relative highs. XLE, CVX, COP and XOM all firm into a weak tape.
Rotation confirmation: if IWM and DIA keep outperforming QQQ on down days, this is rotation. If they start breaking too, it is de-risking.
Earnings punishment severity — ISRG and NFLX were destroyed on results. Reaction size matters more than the prints.
My open XLF long — financials held up better than the index on Friday but the group is not immune to a broad unwind.

key_levels.why: Friday's low and the lowest print since 6/26
key_levels.trigger: a daily close below 740.80 that is not immediately reclaimed

key_levels.why: the floor of the six-session range that just broke
key_levels.trigger: a daily close back above 748 to void the breakdown

key_levels.why: the 6/09 capitulation low is the only structural shelf left below
key_levels.trigger: a break of 686.76 on expanding volume

key_levels.why: Friday's low; the fourth consecutive distribution day ended here
key_levels.trigger: holds 536.81 on a retest, with a close back over 568

key_levels.why: my stop level on the open long, below the 7/8 swing area
key_levels.trigger: any daily close below 55.25 kills the trade

key_levels.why: Friday's low on the breakout session
